meta.description: Case study on iterating an in-app banner in the Tiendanube admin to increase participation in the NubeCommerce survey.
meta.title: UX case study: in-app banner for NubeCommerce | Florencia Frumento
meta.ogTitle: UX case study: in-app banner for NubeCommerce | Florencia Frumento
meta.ogDescription: Case study on iterating an in-app banner in the Tiendanube admin to increase participation in the NubeCommerce survey.
meta.ogImageAlt: Visual preview of the NubeCommerce case study

hero.title: More responses, less friction
hero.claim: How a microcopy and behavior iteration on an in-app banner increased participation in the annual survey for the NubeCommerce report.

metaSummary.roleValue: Content Strategist and Content Squad Lead
metaSummary.channelValue: In-app banner in the admin panel

results.ctrText: increase in CTR on the CTA to complete the survey.
results.responsesText: total consolidated responses in the report survey.
results.windowText: critical window before the survey closed.

related.title: More content decisions applied to product and conversion

situation.label: Situation
situation.title: The report needed more high-quality responses
contentHtml: Every year, Tiendanube publishes NubeCommerce, its annual ecommerce report. To build the 2025 edition, we combined:

• anonymized internal data from more than 65,000 stores in Argentina,
  • the annual merchant survey, and
  • global and regional market sources.

With 15 days left before the deadline, the survey already had a usable base, but the team wanted to expand the sample to strengthen the reading of trends and improve the report's representativeness, both for small businesses and larger mid-market brands.

task.label: Task
task.title: Detect friction without adding interruption
contentHtml: The invitation to respond was being sent by email and through a banner in the store admin panel. The problem was that if someone closed the banner because the moment was inconvenient, they would not see it again.

The hypothesis was that many people might be willing to respond, just not at the exact moment the communication appeared. The task was to reframe the touchpoint so the invitation stayed available without becoming invasive.

action.label: Action
action.title: Iterate the banner behavior and copy
action.intro: Instead of relying on a dismissible appearance, we proposed leaving the invitation fixed as a header inside the admin during the final survey window. The content needed to be clear, direct, and contextual enough to explain why it was worth participating.
action.imageAlt: Recreated in-app banner in the Tiendanube admin with a CTA to answer the NubeCommerce survey
action.imageCaption: Illustrative image recreated from the original banner. Some sensitive data was hidden.
listHtml: • I reviewed the moment in the flow when the invitation appeared.
• I refined the microcopy to guide the action without overexplaining.
• We prioritized a persistent, visible, and less disruptive presence.
• We connected the content decision with real admin usage behavior.
action.beforeText: The banner disappeared permanently, even if the person was interested but needed to postpone their response.
action.afterText: The invitation remained available during the critical period, with a visible CTA and a hierarchy designed to support day-to-day work.

result.label: Result
result.title: More participation from a focused improvement
result.bodyBefore: The iteration achieved a +60% increase in CTA CTR to complete the survey. After three weeks with around 1,900 responses,
result.bodyEmphasis: we reached 3,773 total consolidated responses in the report within 15 days.
result.takeawaysGrowthAlt: NubeCommerce report takeaways page about real growth and marketplaces
result.takeawaysOperationsAlt: NubeCommerce report takeaways page about payments, shipping, and automation
result.takeawaysSupportAlt: NubeCommerce report takeaways page about hybrid support with AI and humans
result.noteBefore: This case shows a small decision with real impact: it wasn't just about changing words or sending more emails, but about
result.noteEmphasis: adjusting content, timing, and interface behavior to match users' real availability
